topic: Scatter Plot and The Best Fit Line

questions.question: What is the purpose of a scatter plot in space exploration?

options.B: To show the relationship between two variables
options.C: To illustrate the life cycle of stars

questions.question: If a scatter plot shows a positive correlation, what can be inferred?

options.A: As one variable increases, the other decreases
options.B: As one variable increases, the other also tends to increase
options.C: There is no relationship between the variables
options.D: The data points are randomly distributed
options.E: The variables are unrelated to space exploration

questions.question: What is the equation of a line of best fit for the data points $(2,3)$, $(4,5)$, and $(6,7)$?
A scientist collects data on the temperature of a planet and its distance from the sun. If the scatter plot shows a negative correlation, what can be concluded?

options.A: As the distance from the sun increases, the temperature also tends to increase
options.B: As the distance from the sun increases, the temperature tends to decrease
options.C: There is no relationship between temperature and distance from the sun
options.D: The data points are randomly distributed
options.E: The temperature is unrelated to the distance from the sun

questions.question: What is the purpose of drawing a line of best fit on a scatter plot?

options.A: To identify outliers in the data
options.B: To visualize the relationship between two variables
options.C: To make predictions based on data trends
options.D: To calculate the mean and median of the data
options.E: To determine the standard deviation of the data

questions.question: A space agency collects data on the altitude of a spacecraft and its velocity. If the scatter plot shows a strong positive correlation, what can be inferred?

options.A: As the altitude increases, the velocity tends to decrease
options.B: As the altitude increases, the velocity tends to increase
options.C: There is no relationship between altitude and velocity
options.D: The data points are randomly distributed
options.E: The velocity is unrelated to the altitude

questions.question: What is the equation of a line of best fit for the data points $(1,2)$, $(3,4)$, and $(5,6)$?
A researcher collects data on the mass of a planet and its orbital period. If the scatter plot shows a weak positive correlation, what can be concluded?

options.A: As the mass increases, the orbital period tends to decrease
options.B: As the mass increases, the orbital period tends to increase
options.C: There is no relationship between mass and orbital period
options.D: The data points are randomly distributed
options.E: The mass is unrelated to the orbital period

questions.question: A space agency collects data on the distance of a spacecraft from Earth and its communication delay. The data points are $(1000,2)$, $(2000,4)$, and $(3000,6)$. Draw a scatter plot and find the equation of the line of best fit. Use the equation to predict the communication delay when the spacecraft is $4000$ km away from Earth.

A scientist collects data on the temperature of a planet and its atmospheric pressure. The data points are $(10,20)$, $(15,30)$, and $(20,40)$. Draw a scatter plot and identify the type of correlation. Use the data to make a prediction about the atmospheric pressure when the temperature is $25$ degrees.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use graph paper to create scatter plots
Tip 2: Remind students to consider the context of the problem when making predictions
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of labeling axes andincluding a title on the scatter plot
